speeches.summary: Media release number 2026-10: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has today published a Conclusions Paper which sets out the final decisions of the Payments System Board (PSB) on the Review of Merchant Card Payment Costs and Surcharging.
speeches.title: Review of Merchant Card Payment Costs and Surcharging – Conclusions Paper

speeches.summary: The Board of Directors of the Bank of Canada today announced the appointment of Marc-André Gosselin and Nicolas Vincent as Deputy Governors, effective May 25 and August 3, 2026, respectively.
speeches.title: Bank of Canada announces the appointment of two Deputy Governors

speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada announced today the departure of Deputy Governor Rhys Mendes on April 10, and the retirement of Deputy Governor Sharon Kozicki on July 15.
speeches.title: Changes to Bank of Canada Governing Council

speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.
speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%

speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada (BoC), RBC Capital Markets, RBC Investor Services, TD Bank Group (TD), and Export Development Canada (EDC) successfully completed Project Samara, a collaborative initiative to evaluate how tokenization and distributed ledger technology (DLT) can improve bond issuance and settlement in a real-world setting.
speeches.title: Bank of Canada, Export Development Canada, RBC and TD successfully complete bond issuance experiment  using distributed ledger technology

speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today issued a revised temporary order to XTM Inc. (XTM). The Bank has authorized XTM to recommence retail payment activities under the supervision of the Monitor appointed today by the Ontario Superior Court of Justice under the Companies' Creditors Arrangement Act, and subject to terms and conditions set out in the Bank's revised order.
speeches.title: Bank of Canada issues revised compliance order to XTM Inc.

speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today issued a temporary order to XTM Inc. (XTM) to immediately cease performing any retail payment activities.
speeches.title: Bank of Canada issues immediate compliance order to XTM Inc.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%

speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada has announced the five finalist teams in the 2025-26 edition of The Governor’s Challenge, a competition where university students simulate the role of advisor to the Bank’s Governing Council.
speeches.title: Bank of Canada announces finalists for the eleventh annual Governor’s Challenge

speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today reduced its target for the overnight rate by 25 basis points to 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.
speeches.title: Bank of Canada lowers policy rate to 2¼%

items.headline: Stocks Wipe Out Gains, Oil Rises as Iran in Focus: Markets Wrap
items.summary: A rebound in oil drove stocks lower as traders awaited confirmation that Iran will join peace talks with the US before a ceasefire expires on Wednesday, with the two sides at odds on issues including access to the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: MUFG Bank Seeks More Acquisitions After $4.3 Billion India Deal
items.summary: MUFG Bank Ltd. is looking for opportunities to make further acquisitions or investments in Asia and elsewhere, according to the new head of the lending unit at Japan’s largest banking group.

items.headline: Oil Rises as Traders Await US-Iran Talks With Truce Winding Down
items.summary: Oil prices rose as traders await potential talks between the US and Iran, with the two sides deadlocked as the deadline to extend a tenuous ceasefire draws close.

items.headline: Magnum Ice Cream Among Biggest Short Seller Bets in Europe
items.summary: Magnum Ice Cream Co., the maker of Ben & Jerry’s, Breyers and Klondike bars, has become one of the most popular short bets in Europe as it grapples with shrinking profits and changing consumer appetites.

items.headline: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Stays at a Trickle of Iran-Aligned Ships
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz remained largely devoid of commercial traffic on Tuesday as Iran continues enforcing a renewed closure of the waterway, leaving only a trickle of small vessels passing through. Most have ties to Tehran but are staying local.

items.headline: Stocks Inch Higher as Traders Mull Earnings, Warsh Testimony
items.summary: US stocks traded higher on Tuesday as investors pored over the latest corporate earnings while assessing the updates on the Iran ceasefire talks as well as Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

items.headline: Trump Urges Judge Not to Let Dimon ‘Escape’ JPMorgan Lawsuit
items.summary: Donald Trump’s lawyer said JPMorgan Chase & Co. Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon can’t “escape liability” for allegedly blacklisting the president after Jan. 6, 2021.

items.headline: Qualcomm’s Best Run in Years Barely Puts a Dent in Brutal Selloff
items.summary: By most objective measures, Qualcomm Inc. appears to be in the midst of a stock market heater.

items.headline: Brad Jacobs on His Big Bet on Building Insulation
items.summary: The famed dealmaker on his latest acquisition.

items.headline: Tiny Iran-Linked Tanker Tests US Blockade With Arabian Sea Move
items.summary: A tiny tanker that’s reportedly transporting liquefied gas from Iran is testing a US blockade of the Islamic Republic’s shipping.

items.headline: Odd Lots: QXO’s Brad Jacobs on His Big Insulation Bet (Podcast)
items.summary: He’s done it again. On Sunday night, building supply company QXO announced that it would be acquiring TopBuild for $17 billion. TopBuild sells and installs insulation for both the residential and commercial markets. For Brad Jacobs, the CEO of QXO, this is just the latest in a lifetime of deals he’s made. In fact, he’s made over 500 deals in his life across numerous public companies that he’s founded, most of which have XO somewhere in the ticker. Brad’s companies all tend to be highly focused o

items.headline: Ugandan Banks Balk at New ‘Foreign Agents’ Registration Law
items.summary: Ugandan banks are seeking an exemption from proposed legislation requiring all businesses and individuals that receive funds from outside the country to be registered, arguing that the measure will negatively impact their ability to raise capital.

items.headline: Ukraine Willing to Delay Access to EU Benefits, Deputy PM Kachka Says
items.summary: Ukraine is open to delay access to the EU Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) funding initially, however, it may become part of negotiations over the next seven-year budget, which would begin in 2034, Ukraine’s Deputy Prime Minister Taras Kachka says in an interview on Bloomberg TV. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Equifax Sticks to 2026 Outlook as Iran War Brings Caution
items.summary: Equifax Inc. said the Iran war prompted more caution about the outlook for the rest of the year, even after a surge in mortgage applications drove first-quarter revenue growth to the fastest in more than four years.

items.headline: Le Pen’s Energy Plan for France Called a ‘Disaster’ by Engie CEO
items.summary: A plan by Marine Le Pen’s far-right party to scrap renewables and the energy regulator would be a disaster for France, the CEO of giant utility Engie SA said, in one of the sharpest rebukes from a business leader ahead of next year’s presidential election.

items.headline: Riskiest Junk Debt Is Lagging as Investors Continue to Fret About Software Firms’ AI Future
items.summary: Investors, worried about artificial intelligence disrupting software companies, are skirting the most-troubled US junk debt even as they embrace risk in other markets.

items.headline: US Natural Gas Ticks Down on Mild Weather, Growing Surplus
items.summary: US natural gas futures fell slightly as weather forecasts shifted warmer, indicating slightly lower demand for the heating and power-plant fuel. Expectations that domestic stockpiles will move higher above the five-year average may also be putting pressure on prices.

items.headline: Wall Street Banks Flag Risks for Kenya’s Shilling as War Rages On
items.summary: Kenya’s shilling is among the most vulnerable currencies in Africa, according to strategists at major banks, who predict further weakness as elevated oil prices pile pressure on the economy.

items.headline: Saudi IT Firm to Press Ahead with IPO Despite War Uncertainty
items.summary: Saudi Arabian information technology services firm Dar Al Balad Business Solutions Co. is pressing ahead with plans to list on the kingdom’s stock exchange despite the disruptions caused by the regional conflict.

items.headline: Treasuries Weaken Before Warsh Testifies for Fed Chair Role
items.summary: Treasuries fell as strong economic data prompted traders to scale back rate-cut expectations ahead of testimony from Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh.

items.headline: BMO, Teck, TC Energy CEOs to Advise Carney on US Strategy on New Panel
items.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney is turning to some of Canada’s most prominent business executives, including Bank of Montreal’s Darryl White, to give him advice on how to handle Canada’s economic relationship with the US.

items.headline: German Pension VBL Shifts €7 Billion Housing Portfolio Into Fund
items.summary: German public-sector pension fund Versorgungsanstalt des Bundes und der Länder, or VBL, is transferring its residential property portfolio worth around €7 billion ($8.2 billion) into a new fund structure.

items.headline: Billionaire Fredriksen’s Firm Faces $1 Billion Claim After Failed Fraud Trial
items.summary: Two former executives at billionaire John Fredriksen’s oil trading firm sued for about $1 billion in over allegations that the shipping tycoon’s failed “vindictive” lawsuit brought them to the “brink of financial ruin.”

items.headline: Core Scientific Adds to AI Junk-Bond Wave With $3.3 Billion Deal
items.summary: Core Scientific Inc. is looking to raise $3.3 billion from a junk-bond sale, joining a wave of high-yield issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

items.headline: Romania’s Premier Faces Ultimatum to Quit in Escalating Crisis
items.summary: The biggest party in Romania’s ruling coalition warned it will withdraw ministers from the government, raising pressure on Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan to step down and pushing the four-way alliance further to the brink of collapse.

items.headline: Polish Arms Maker Set for Record Sales on Defense Spending Spree
items.summary: Poland’s biggest defense group is targeting another record year as the European Union’s largest land force ramps up spending to counter threats from Russia and tap into new EU funding.

items.headline: Russia Set to Ramp Up Oil Flows as Impact of Drone Strikes Fades
items.summary: Moscow has resumed loading operations at key ports, setting the stage for a rebound in exports

items.headline: US Retail Sales Soar in March Led by Spending on Gas
items.summary: Americans spent 15.5% more on gas in March as prices surged due to the war in Iran. Retail sales rose by 1.7% topping analyst estimates. Enda Curran reports from Washington. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: SEC Monitoring ‘Emerging Pressures’ in Private Credit Space
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission is closely monitoring the “emerging pressures” in the private credit market as redemption requests persist amid rising default-rate projections, Chairman Paul Atkins said in prepared remarks.

items.headline: Switch Lands $2.6 Billion of Bank Pledges to Power Data Centers
items.summary: Switch Inc. gathered $2.6 billion of bank pledges to help the data-center operator procure electricity, a mounting challenge for the booming industry and one of its biggest costs.
